ab: Slaqi ku siida mniq nami Hori, meuyas patas do thjil Tay-cong da, bitaq sayang gisu nami mniq alang paru na.
ch: 小時候我們家住在埔里，上學以後，就搬到了台中，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Puli. After I started school, we moved to Taichung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Meuyas ku pyasan paru do, mnegaaw ku kingal quri knkla kndusan seejiq tnpusu slhayan, klaan saw seejiq tnpusu Taywan ga niqan egu kleegan ka knkla kndusan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Kiya ni, sngayan rbagan nii do, yaku ni qbsuran snaw ga musa nami alang smiling rudan, mkmkla quri kndusan mnan nanaq.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Kingal wada mspac kmxalan hngkwasan na rudan, ida mbiyax bi qtaan na.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Smiling ku hyaan, piya ka klaun su rmngaw kari hug? Ida mkla bi rmngaw kari Seejiq, mkla rmngaw kari Tayal, kari Tanah Tunux uri ka hiya.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的賽德克語說得很流利，也會說泰雅語與日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Seejiq, as well as Atayal and Japanese.

ab: Kla kndadax slaqi siida, smnluhay kari Tanah Tunux, Tayal ka qrijil na, qpqhan na hiya o hbaraw bi ka klaan na seejiq Khilang, kiya ni egu bi ka klaan na rmngaw kari.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是泰雅族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Atayal, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Smiling nami siida, smnru egu bi quri knklaan rdrudan sbiyaw ka rudan nii.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Ana nanaq ini lxani mkla qmbahang o, ida nami wada sru-ing ruwan qnawal baga, tduwa saun qmbahang duri.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Slaqi ku siida, ini ku kla kndusan rudan sbiyaw, sqaun mu saw pneiyah ku seejiq tnpusu ka yaku.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Egu ka klaun do pqniqan nanaq lnglungan da, mqaras ku balay, yasa Seejiq ku Balay ka yaku.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為賽德克族，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Seejiq is unique and something to be proud of.
